name: simplex-chat
description: SimpleX - the first messaging network operating without user identifiers of any kind - 100% private by design! iOS, Android and desktop apps 📱!
licenseName: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

curation.summary: SimpleX Chat is a privacy-focused messaging platform that operates without any user identifiers, providing 100% private communication by design. Built in Haskell, it offers iOS, Android, and desktop applications with end-to-end encryption using the Double Ratchet algorithm.
curation.whyNotable: It's the first messaging network to completely eliminate user identifiers (including phone numbers or emails), solving the metadata privacy problem that affects even encrypted messengers like Signal. Recommended by privacy guides and security audited by Trail of Bits.
strengths: Unique architecture with no user identifiers - solves metadata privacy
Strong end-to-end encryption with Double Ratchet algorithm
Cross-platform support (iOS, Android, desktop)
Active development with frequent releases and security audits
Open source with AGPL-3.0 license
limitations: Haskell codebase may limit contributor accessibility
Network effects require critical mass of users
No examples provided for developers
useCases: Private messaging for privacy-conscious individuals
Secure communications for journalists and activists
Corporate communications requiring metadata protection
Anyone seeking alternatives to phone-number-based messengers
curation.targetAudience: Privacy-conscious users, journalists, activists, security researchers, and anyone concerned about metadata surveillance
curation.oneLiner: The first messaging platform with no user identifiers, providing 100% private communication by design.

name: agency-agents
description: A complete AI agency at your fingertips - From frontend wizards to Reddit community ninjas, from whimsy injectors to reality checkers. Each agent is a specialized expert with personality, processes, and proven deliverables.

curation.summary: The Agency is a curated collection of specialized AI agent personas — each with distinct expertise, personality, and defined workflows — designed to be installed into AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and others. It provides a standardized roster of 'specialist' agents (e.g., frontend wizards, community managers, reality checkers) as markdown-based agent definitions with shell installation scripts and a companion native desktop app.
curation.whyNotable: With over 123K stars and a companion native app, this project has tapped into a massive demand for specialized AI agent personas that work across multiple AI coding tools. Its cross-platform compatibility and curated, personality-driven approach to AI agent definitions make it a practical resource for anyone looking to enhance their AI-assisted workflows without building agents from scratch.
strengths: Cross-tool compatibility supporting Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini CLI, and more with a single agent definition set
Well-curated, personality-driven agent personas with defined workflows and deliverables rather than generic prompt templates
Strong community adoption with 123K+ stars, 87 contributors, and a native desktop app for frictionless installation
Excellent README with clear quick-start instructions, badges, and visual structure
limitations: Fundamentally a curated prompt/persona library rather than a functional agent framework — relies entirely on external AI tools to operate
No tests or quality validation mechanisms for measuring agent effectiveness or consistency across updates
Shell-based project with no Docker support limits reproducibility and deployment flexibility
useCases: Enhancing AI coding assistants with specialized expert personas for domains like frontend development, community management, or QA
Teams wanting to standardize agent behavior across multiple AI coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, etc.)
Developers looking for ready-made agent personalities to reduce prompt engineering effort in their AI workflows
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Gemini) who want to quickly deploy specialized agent personas without crafting their own system prompts
curation.oneLiner: A massively popular curated roster of personality-driven AI agent definitions installable across all major AI coding tools with one click

name: cupy
description: NumPy & SciPy for GPU

curation.summary: CuPy is a GPU-accelerated array library that serves as a drop-in replacement for NumPy and SciPy, enabling high-performance computing on NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm platforms. It provides a familiar API for numerical computing while leveraging GPU parallelism for significant speedups.
curation.whyNotable: CuPy is a mature, widely-adopted project that bridges the gap between CPU-based scientific computing and GPU acceleration, with extensive compatibility with NumPy/SciPy APIs and support for both CUDA and ROCm backends.
strengths: Drop-in replacement for NumPy/SciPy with high API compatibility
Supports both NVIDIA CUDA and AMD ROCm platforms
Extensive documentation, tutorials, and examples
Active development with frequent releases and large community
limitations: Installation can be complex due to CUDA/ROCm version dependencies
Not all NumPy/SciPy functions are implemented yet
Performance gains depend on problem size and GPU memory availability
useCases: Accelerating numerical computations in scientific research
Deep learning data preprocessing and custom operations
High-performance computing tasks requiring GPU parallelism
curation.targetAudience: Data scientists, researchers, and engineers working with large-scale numerical computations who need GPU acceleration without leaving the Python ecosystem.
curation.oneLiner: CuPy brings NumPy/SciPy to the GPU with drop-in compatibility and multi-platform support.

name: FluidVoice
description: Fastest and only macOS Dictation app with on-device STT and custom trained AI enhancement model. A local Wispr Flow alternative. ⭐ helps a ton :) Windows & iOS waitlist open. Linux soon.

curation.summary: FluidVoice is an open-source macOS dictation application that provides fast, fully on-device speech-to-text using models like Parakeet, Whisper, and Nemotron, combined with a proprietary 'Fluid Intelligence' AI enhancement model for smart formatting and post-processing. It's positioned as a local, privacy-first alternative to cloud-based dictation tools like Wispr Flow, with no data leaving the user's Mac.
curation.whyNotable: One of the few open-source, on-device dictation apps achieving real-time performance with local AI enhancement — no cloud APIs required. Its rapid release cadence (17 releases in ~9 months), strong community adoption (5,500+ stars), and Homebrew distribution make it unusually polished for an open-source desktop AI application.
strengths: Fully on-device inference with no cloud dependency, strong privacy proposition
Impressive release velocity and active development with 17 releases in 6 months
Clean distribution via Homebrew cask, making installation trivially easy for macOS users
Supports multiple STT backends (Parakeet, Whisper, Nemotron, Apple Speech) giving users flexibility
Strong community traction with 5,500+ stars and growing
limitations: No automated test suite, raising concerns about regression safety and code quality assurance
The 'Fluid Intelligence' AI enhancement component is closed-source/proprietary despite the GPL license, creating an unusual open-core tension
Limited to macOS currently — iOS, Windows, and Linux support announced but not yet available
useCases: Writers and professionals who need fast, accurate dictation without cloud privacy concerns
Users seeking a free, open-source alternative to Wispr Flow, Dragon, or similar paid dictation tools
Developers who want a local dictation engine to integrate or extend for their own workflows
curation.targetAudience: Mac users who need frequent voice-to-text dictation — writers, developers, accessibility users, and anyone prioritizing privacy by keeping speech data on-device
curation.oneLiner: A blazing-fast, privacy-first macOS dictation app with on-device AI enhancement — the open-source local alternative to Wispr Flow

name: maigret
description: 🕵️‍♂️ Collect a dossier on a person by username from 3000+ sites

curation.summary: Maigret is an OSINT reconnaissance tool that collects a dossier on a person by checking their username across 3,000+ websites, gathering publicly available profile information without requiring API keys. It features AI profiling capabilities and is designed for cybersecurity professionals, investigators, and penetration testers.
curation.whyNotable: With 34,800+ stars and active development since 2020, Maigret has become one of the most popular OSINT tools for username enumeration, offering unmatched site coverage and a self-contained scraping approach that doesn't rely on third-party APIs.
strengths: Massive coverage of 3,000+ sites for username checking
No API keys required for data collection
Active development with CI, tests, Docker support, and regular releases
AI profiling capabilities for enhanced analysis
Large community with 75 contributors and extensive star history
limitations: Web scraping approach is inherently fragile and breaks when sites update
No usage examples provided in the repository
Potential for false positives/negatives in account detection
useCases: OSINT investigations and digital footprint analysis
Penetration testing and red team reconnaissance
Personal security auditing and brand monitoring
Law enforcement and private investigator research
curation.targetAudience: OSINT researchers, penetration testers, cybersecurity professionals, and digital investigators
curation.oneLiner: A comprehensive OSINT tool that checks 3,000+ sites for username availability and compiles public profile dossiers without API keys

name: openpilot
description: openpilot is an operating system for robotics. Currently, it upgrades the driver assistance system on 300+ supported cars.

curation.summary: openpilot is an open-source driver assistance system that upgrades the ADAS capabilities of 300+ supported cars using comma.ai's hardware. It leverages computer vision and deep learning to provide lane keeping, adaptive cruise control, and other autonomous driving features on consumer vehicles.
curation.whyNotable: It's one of the most successful open-source autonomous driving projects, with a massive community of 732 contributors and real-world deployment across hundreds of car models. Its open-source nature in a space dominated by proprietary systems from Tesla and Waymo makes it uniquely accessible for research and customization.
strengths: Supports 300+ car models with real-world deployment
Large active community with 732 contributors and 62K+ stars
Open-source alternative to proprietary ADAS systems
Well-documented with dedicated docs site and roadmap
limitations: No automated tests in the repository
Requires proprietary comma.ai hardware (comma four)
Limited to driver assistance rather than full autonomy
No Docker support or examples for easy onboarding
useCases: Upgrading factory driver assistance in supported consumer vehicles
Research platform for autonomous driving algorithms
Custom ADAS development and experimentation
Fleet management with enhanced driver assistance
curation.targetAudience: Automotive enthusiasts, ADAS researchers, robotics engineers, and developers interested in real-world autonomous driving systems
curation.oneLiner: An open-source driver assistance OS bringing AI-powered autonomy to 300+ consumer cars

name: free-for-dev
description: A list of SaaS, PaaS and IaaS offerings that have free tiers of interest to devops and infradev

name: logto
description: 🧑‍🚀 Authentication and authorization infrastructure for SaaS and AI apps, built on OIDC and OAuth 2.1 with multi-tenancy, SSO, and RBAC.

curation.summary: Logto is an open-source authentication and authorization infrastructure designed for SaaS and AI applications. It provides a comprehensive implementation of OIDC, OAuth 2.1, and SAML with built-in support for multi-tenancy, enterprise SSO, RBAC, and multi-factor authentication. The project offers both cloud-hosted and self-hosted options with SDKs for over 30 frameworks.
curation.whyNotable: Logto stands out for its modern developer experience, comprehensive feature set, and explicit support for AI agent architectures through Model Context Protocol (MCP). With 13.5k stars and active development from 105 contributors, it has quickly become a popular alternative to commercial auth solutions like Auth0, offering enterprise-grade features without vendor lock-in.
strengths: Comprehensive protocol support (OIDC, OAuth 2.1, SAML) with built-in multi-tenancy and RBAC
Native support for AI agent architectures via Model Context Protocol (MCP)
Excellent developer experience with SDKs for 30+ frameworks and pre-built UI components
Active development with 8 releases in 6 months and 105 contributors
Flexible deployment options including fully managed cloud and self-hosted Docker
limitations: Not fundamentally an AI/ML project despite marketing toward AI applications
Authentication remains complex under the hood despite simplified APIs
Missing comprehensive examples and test coverage indicators in repository metadata
useCases: Implementing authentication and authorization for multi-tenant SaaS applications
Adding enterprise SSO and RBAC to existing applications without building auth from scratch
Securing AI agent architectures and MCP-based systems with standardized auth flows
Rapid prototyping of applications requiring social login, passwordless, and MFA capabilities
curation.targetAudience: SaaS developers, AI application builders, and engineering teams needing enterprise-grade authentication infrastructure without the complexity of implementing OIDC/OAuth 2.1 from scratch
curation.oneLiner: Modern open-source auth infrastructure that simplifies OIDC/OAuth 2.1 with built-in multi-tenancy, enterprise SSO, and native support for AI agent architectures via MCP.

name: ai-berkshire
description: AI 时代的伯克希尔：基于 Claude Code / Codex 的价值投资研究框架。巴菲特·芒格·段永平·李录四大师方法论 + 多Agent并行研究。| AI-era Berkshire: a value investing research framework built for Claude Code / Codex. 4 masters' methodologies + multi-agent adversarial analysis.

curation.summary: AI Berkshire is a structured value investing research framework that operationalizes the methodologies of four legendary investors (Buffett, Munger, Duan Yongping, Li Lu) as multi-agent AI skills for Claude Code and Codex. It enforces disciplined, reproducible investment analysis through adversarial multi-perspective evaluation, anti-bias mechanisms, and precise financial calculations. The project claims impressive real-world investment returns (+69% in 2024, +66% in 2025) as validation of the framework.
curation.whyNotable: It transforms generic AI financial analysis into a disciplined, multi-master adversarial research system with built-in anti-bias checks and forced decision outputs—a genuinely novel application of AI agents to value investing that bridges professional investment methodology with LLM capabilities. The claimed real-world track record and massive community adoption (8.5K+ stars) indicate strong practical resonance.
strengths: Innovative multi-perspective adversarial analysis framework that systematizes four distinct investment master philosophies into structured AI agent workflows
Strong emphasis on anti-bias mechanisms (information richness ratings, Munger-style inversion, quick veto checklists, anti-consensus checks) that address real LLM failure modes
Exceptionally clear README with compelling narrative, concrete examples, and demonstrated real-world investment results
Enforces reproducible, standardized research output format enabling cross-company comparison and temporal tracking
limitations: No CI, tests, Docker, or examples directory—lacks standard software production infrastructure despite being a popular project
Historical investment returns are unverifiable single-source claims and cannot validate the methodology rigorously; survivorship bias is unaddressed
useCases: Individual investors using Claude Code or Codex to conduct systematic, multi-framework stock analysis with structured decision outputs
Investment research teams seeking reproducible, standardized analysis templates that enforce anti-bias discipline
Learning tool for understanding and applying value investing philosophies from Buffett, Munger, Duan Yongping, and Li Lu through AI-assisted research
curation.targetAudience: Individual value investors and small investment research teams who use Claude Code or Codex and want to apply structured, multi-master investment methodologies with AI assistance
curation.oneLiner: A multi-agent value investing framework that turns Claude Code into a disciplined, four-master adversarial research team with built-in anti-bias mechanisms

name: video-use
description: Edit videos with coding agents

name: VulnClaw
description: 基于 AI Agent + MCP 工具链 + 渗透 Skill 编排， 配合大语言模型， 自然语言输入 → 自动完成「信息收集 → 漏洞发现 → 漏洞利用 → 报告生成」全流程。

curation.summary: VulnClaw is an AI-driven penetration testing CLI tool that combines LLM Agent architecture with MCP toolchain and penetration testing Skill orchestration. It accepts natural language input and autonomously executes the full security assessment workflow: information gathering, vulnerability discovery, exploitation, and report generation.
curation.whyNotable: Introduces a goal-driven solving engine using blackboard graph state space search and OODA loop for penetration testing, replacing naive fixed-round loops. Features evidence-based anti-hallucination gates that require tool output verification, 21 specialized penetration skills, and support for 13 LLM providers — a structured approach to preventing AI agents from 'spinning in circles' during security assessments.
strengths: Goal-driven solving engine with blackboard graph state space search avoids fixed-round loops and 'spinning in circles'
Evidence-based anti-hallucination gates require real tool output verification before accepting conclusions
Rich skill ecosystem: 21 penetration skills with 180 reference docs covering CTF, OSINT, web exploitation
Broad LLM provider support (13 providers) with OpenAI-compatible protocol
MCP toolchain integration for extensible tool calling (fetch, memory, chrome-devtools, burp)
Web UI mode and structured Markdown report generation with runnable Python PoC scripts
limitations: Primary documentation and community are Chinese-centric, limiting global accessibility
Python execution tool is explicitly high-risk and not a strong isolation sandbox
Relatively new project (created April 2026) with evolving architecture and limited track record
Niche domain focus means smaller community compared to general-purpose agent frameworks
useCases: Authorized penetration testing with natural language task descriptions
CTF competition assistance across Web, Crypto, and Misc categories
Security education and red team training exercises
Automated vulnerability assessment with structured reporting
curation.targetAudience: Security researchers, penetration testers, CTF players, red team operators, and cybersecurity educators who want AI-assisted autonomous security assessments
curation.oneLiner: AI penetration testing agent using blackboard graph state space search to autonomously conduct security assessments from natural language, with evidence-based anti-hallucination gates

name: council-of-high-intelligence
description: 18 AI personas deliberate your hardest decisions across multiple LLM providers. Aristotle, Feynman, Kahneman, Torvalds & more — structured multi-round deliberation with genuine model diversity. One command: /council

name: Vibe-Trading
description: "Vibe-Trading: Your Personal Trading Agent"

curation.summary: Vibe-Trading is an open-source framework for building personal AI trading agents, leveraging large language models (LLMs) for comprehensive trading capabilities including multi-agent orchestration and backtesting. It provides tools for developing algorithmic trading strategies with a focus on integration with modern AI technologies.
curation.whyNotable: With over 17k GitHub stars and active development, Vibe-Trading stands out for its innovative fusion of AI agents with financial trading, offering a powerful platform for both hobbyists and professionals in quantitative finance to create and test personalized trading bots.
strengths: High community adoption with 17k+ stars and regular releases indicating strong interest
Integrates LLMs and multi-agent systems for advanced trading strategy development
Supports Docker and CI/CD for deployment and scalability
limitations: Lack of mentioned tests may impact reliability for production use
Potential steep learning curve for users new to both trading and AI agent concepts
useCases: Building and backtesting personalized AI-powered trading bots
Educational tool for learning about AI integration in quantitative finance
Research platform for developing and testing algorithmic trading strategies with agent-based approaches
curation.targetAudience: Developers, quantitative traders, and researchers interested in AI-driven trading systems
curation.oneLiner: An AI-powered multi-agent framework for building personalized trading bots with LLM integration and comprehensive backtesting capabilities.

name: tolaria
description: Desktop app to manage markdown knowledge bases
licenseName: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

curation.summary: Tolaria is a cross-platform desktop application (macOS, Windows, Linux) built with TypeScript for managing markdown knowledge bases. It supports personal knowledge management, second-brain workflows, and organizing company documentation as context for AI systems, with the creator using it to manage 10,000+ notes.
curation.whyNotable: With over 18,000 stars, 100 releases in 6 months, and 24 contributors, Tolaria demonstrates remarkable community traction and development velocity in the knowledge management space. Its explicit positioning as an AI context organizer for company docs and AI assistant memory gives it a forward-looking angle beyond traditional note-taking.
strengths: Impressive production maturity with CI, code coverage, CodeScene health monitoring, and 100 releases in 6 months
Cross-platform desktop support (macOS, Windows, Linux) with a clean, polished UI
Strong community adoption with 18K+ stars and active contributor base of 24
Explicit AI-context use cases (organizing company docs for AI, storing assistant memory) position it for the AI-native workflow era
limitations: Competitive landscape is crowded with established players like Obsidian, Logseq, and Notion that have larger ecosystems and plugin communities
No Docker support or web-based alternative limits deployment flexibility for team/enterprise scenarios
useCases: Operating a personal second brain and knowledge management system
Organizing company documentation as structured context for AI assistants
Storing procedures and memory for AI agents and assistants
curation.targetAudience: Knowledge workers, developers, and AI practitioners who manage large markdown-based note collections and want a dedicated desktop experience for personal knowledge management with AI integration potential
curation.oneLiner: A highly polished, cross-platform markdown knowledge base manager with strong AI-context organization capabilities and exceptional release cadence

name: OpenMontage
description: World's first open-source, agentic video production system. 12 pipelines, 52 tools, 500+ agent skills. Turn your AI coding assistant into a full video production studio.
licenseName: GNU Affero General Public License v3.0

curation.summary: OpenMontage is an open-source agentic video production system that orchestrates AI coding assistants through 12 pipelines, 52 tools, and 500+ agent skills to automate the full video production lifecycle — from research and scripting to asset generation, editing, and final composition. It distinguishes itself by enabling real video production using free stock footage and open archives rather than merely animating still images.
curation.whyNotable: With over 32,000 stars and GitHub trending recognition, OpenMontage represents a bold convergence of agentic AI and creative production, offering one of the most comprehensive open-source attempts to fully automate video production through AI agent orchestration.
strengths: Comprehensive agentic architecture with 500+ skills, 52 tools, and 12 pipelines covering the full video production lifecycle
Well-structured README with clear demos, video examples, and organized documentation
Active development with CI/CD and test infrastructure in place
Strong community traction with 32k+ stars and active discussions
limitations: No formal releases or Docker support despite ambitious scope
No examples directory provided for new users
Very new project (created March 2026) with only 15 contributors, raising questions about long-term sustainability

AI-assisted social media video content creation
Educational and explainer video generation from text prompts
Rapid prototyping of video concepts using agent-orchestrated workflows
curation.targetAudience: Content creators, video producers, AI enthusiasts, and developers interested in agentic AI applications for creative workflows
curation.oneLiner: An agentic AI system that turns coding assistants into full video production studios with 500+ skills orchestrating research, scripting, asset generation, and editing.

name: codebase-memory-mcp
description: High-performance code intelligence MCP server. Indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph — average repo in milliseconds. 158 languages, sub-ms queries, 99% fewer tokens. Single static binary, zero dependencies.

curation.summary: A high-performance MCP server written in C that indexes codebases into a persistent knowledge graph using tree-sitter AST analysis across 158 languages. It provides sub-millisecond structural code queries to AI coding agents, reducing token usage by 99% compared to file-by-file exploration.
curation.whyNotable: Delivers exceptional performance (average repo indexed in milliseconds, Linux kernel in 3 minutes) as a single zero-dependency static tree-sitter binary with Hybrid LSP semantic resolution. Backed by published research showing 83% answer quality with 10× fewer tokens, and trusted by 25K+ stars with strong security posture (SLSA 3, OpenSSF, VirusTotal).
strengths: Blazing fast performance: millisecond indexing and sub-millisecond queries
Single static binary with zero dependencies, cross-platform (macOS/Linux/Windows)
Broad language support: 158 languages with Hybrid LSP for 9 major languages
Strong security and trust: SLSA 3, OpenSSF Scorecard, VirusTotal scanning
Research-backed with published arXiv paper and real-world benchmarks
Wide agent compatibility: works with 11 AI coding agents out of the box
limitations: No Docker support for containerized workflows
No examples directory for onboarding new users
Written in C, which may limit community contribution velocity
148 open issues suggest active but potentially unstable development
useCases: AI coding agents needing fast structural code navigation and cross-references
Large codebase exploration without expensive token-heavy file reading
Call chain analysis and dependency mapping for LLM context
HTTP route discovery and cross-service link analysis in microservices
curation.targetAudience: Developers using AI coding agents (Claude Code, Cursor, Aider, Codex, etc.) who work with large codebases and want faster, more token-efficient code intelligence
curation.oneLiner: Zero-dependency C MCP server that indexes any codebase into a knowledge graph in milliseconds, giving AI coding agents sub-ms structural queries with 99% fewer tokens.

name: design.md
description: A format specification for describing a visual identity to coding agents. DESIGN.md gives agents a persistent, structured understanding of a design system.

curation.summary: DESIGN.md is a format specification from Google Labs that lets developers describe visual design systems in a structured, AI-agent-readable format. It combines YAML front matter for machine-readable design tokens with markdown prose for human-readable design rationale, enabling AI coding agents to produce UIs that faithfully adhere to a design system.
curation.whyNotable: This project addresses a critical gap in the AI-assisted development workflow: how to give coding agents persistent, structured understanding of design systems rather than relying on ad-hoc prompting. Backed by Google Labs, it has achieved remarkable adoption (24k+ stars in just a few months) and includes practical CLI tooling for linting (including WCAG contrast checks) and diffing design system versions.
strengths: Elegant specification design that elegantly bridges machine-readable tokens with human-readable prose in a single file
Practical CLI tooling with lint (including WCAG contrast validation) and diff commands that output structured JSON for agent consumption
Massive community adoption (24k+ stars) indicating strong demand for structured design-to-agent communication
Well-structured specification with clear separation between normative tokens and contextual prose
Backed by Google Labs with active development (4 releases in 6 months, 17 contributors)
limitations: No automated test suite, which is a gap for a specification project that needs high reliability
Effectiveness depends on AI coding agents supporting and correctly interpreting the format — ecosystem adoption is still nascent
Alpha-stage specification may see breaking changes as the format matures
useCases: Providing AI coding assistants (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) with persistent design system context so generated UIs match brand guidelines
Version-controlling design systems with diffable, reviewable format changes across releases
Enforcing WCAG accessibility compliance and design token consistency via automated linting in CI pipelines
curation.targetAudience: Frontend developers and design engineers who use AI coding agents and need to ensure generated code adheres to established design systems; also design system teams maintaining cross-platform visual identity
curation.oneLiner: A Google-backed specification that gives AI coding agents a persistent, structured understanding of your design system through a single markdown file.

name: no-mistakes
description: git push no-mistakes

curation.summary: no-mistakes is a Go-based local git proxy that intercepts `git push` commands and runs an AI-driven validation pipeline (review, test, docs, lint) in an isolated worktree before forwarding branches to the real remote. It automatically applies safe fixes, escalates ambiguous issues to the developer, and opens clean PRs only after all checks pass.
curation.whyNotable: It creatively reimagines the git push workflow by inserting an AI-powered quality gate that works agent-agnostically with Claude, Codex, Copilot, and others, making it a unique intersection of developer tooling and AI agents that prevents mistakes before they reach the remote.
strengths: Agent-agnostic design supporting multiple AI coding assistants (Claude, Codex, Copilot, etc.)
Non-blocking architecture using disposable worktrees so developers' work is never disrupted
Seamless integration into existing git workflow — just push to a different remote and get clean PRs automatically
Rapid release cadence (77 releases in ~3 months) with cross-platform support and CI automation
limitations: No unit or integration tests listed in the repository, raising concerns about reliability and regression safety
Relies heavily on external AI agents for validation quality — behavior may be inconsistent across agent providers and API versions
useCases: Individual developers wanting to catch bugs, lint errors, and documentation gaps before opening PRs
Teams enforcing automated AI-powered code review gates in their git workflow
AI coding agent workflows where agents complete tasks and then gate their output through automated validation before human review
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding assistants who want to prevent sloppy or broken code from reaching their shared repositories
curation.oneLiner: An AI-powered git push proxy that gates every push behind automated review, test, and lint — so only clean code gets a PR.

name: ai-website-cloner-template
description: Clone any website with one command using AI coding agents

curation.summary: A reusable Next.js template that uses AI coding agents (primarily Claude Code) to reverse-engineer any website into a clean, modern codebase with a single command. It extracts design tokens, assets, and component specs, then dispatches parallel builders to reconstruct the site using shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS.
curation.whyNotable: With over 25,000 stars, this project taps into the growing trend of AI-assisted development by making website cloning trivially simple. It demonstrates a practical, high-impact use case for AI coding agents beyond code generation — automating the tedious process of recreating existing designs in a modern stack.
strengths: Extremely simple one-command workflow for cloning any website
Produces clean, modern Next.js output with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS
Well-documented with clear quick-start guide and demo video
Active development with 5 releases in 6 months and recent commits
Supports parallel builders for faster reconstruction
limitations: No automated tests despite having CI
Primarily optimized for Claude Code with limited multi-agent support
Very new project (created March 2026) with limited track record
No examples directory to showcase output quality
useCases: Rapidly prototyping new websites by cloning and modifying existing designs
Migrating legacy or non-React websites to a modern Next.js stack
Extracting design systems and component libraries from reference sites
Creating starter templates for client projects based on competitor designs
curation.targetAudience: Web developers and designers who want to quickly replicate or migrate website designs using AI coding agents, particularly those already using Claude Code
curation.oneLiner: Turn any website into a clean Next.js codebase with a single AI agent command

curation.summary: LLM-powered multi-market stock analysis system that aggregates real-time market data, news, and technical indicators across A股, 港股, 美股, and other Asian markets to generate AI-driven decision dashboards with automated notifications via WeChat, Feishu, Telegram, and other platforms.

strengths: Comprehensive multi-market coverage including A股, 港股, 美股, 日股, 韩股, and 台股
Rich notification ecosystem supporting WeChat, Feishu, Telegram, Discord, Slack, and email

Web dashboard with backtesting, portfolio tracking, and dark/light theme support

Multi-market portfolio monitoring with AI-generated risk alerts and buy/sell signals
Agent-based interactive stock questioning using 15+ built-in strategies (moving averages, Chan theory, wave theory, etc.)

name: orca
description: Orca is the ADE for working with a fleet of parallel agents. Run any coding agent with your own subscription. Available on desktop and mobile.

curation.summary: Orca is an Agent Development Environment (ADE) that enables developers to run multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Pi) in parallel, each in isolated git worktrees. It features a desktop application for macOS, Windows, and Linux, plus a mobile companion app for remote monitoring and steering of agents.
curation.whyNotable: Orca introduces a novel approach to AI-assisted development by orchestrating multiple agents simultaneously with isolated worktrees, allowing developers to fan a single prompt across several agents and merge the best result. The mobile companion app for remote agent monitoring is a unique differentiator in the AI dev tool space.
strengths: Parallel agent execution with isolated git worktrees for safe experimentation
Cross-platform support with desktop and mobile companion apps
Multi-agent orchestration supporting Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, and Pi
Active development with 100 releases and 146 contributors
limitations: High open issue count (391) relative to project maturity
Very recent project (created March 2026) with limited production track record
Requires separate subscriptions for each AI agent backend
useCases: A/B testing multiple AI coding agents on the same prompt to find optimal solutions
Remote monitoring and steering of long-running AI agents via mobile
Large-scale parallel code generation with isolated worktrees
Comparing outputs from different AI agents before merging
curation.targetAudience: AI-powered developers and teams who work with multiple AI coding agents and want to orchestrate them in parallel
curation.oneLiner: An agent development environment that orchestrates multiple AI coding agents in parallel with isolated worktrees and mobile monitoring.

name: Agent-Reach
description: Give your AI agent eyes to see the entire internet. Read & search Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu — one CLI, zero API fees.

curation.summary: Agent Reach is a Python CLI tool that gives AI agents unified internet access across Twitter, Reddit, YouTube, GitHub, Bilibili, XiaoHongShu, and more — all without paid API fees. It uses multi-backend routing with automatic failover so agents can read, search, and scrape content from these platforms through a single interface.
curation.whyNotable: With nearly 50K stars, Agent Reach solves a real pain point: AI agents are blind to most of the internet due to paywalls, anti-bot measures, and fragmented APIs. Its multi-backend routing approach (e.g., switching from yt-dlp to bili-cli when Bilibili blocks scraping) provides resilience that individual scrapers lack, and the one-sentence install experience via AI agents is a clever distribution innovation.
strengths: Multi-backend routing with automatic failover ensures resilience when platforms block scrapers
Zero API fees with privacy-first design (cookies stay local)
One-sentence install via AI agent instructions is a novel and frictionless distribution model
Built-in diagnostics (agent-reach doctor) for troubleshooting
Active development with 30 contributors and 7 releases in 6 months, rapidly adapting to platform changes
limitations: Web scraping is inherently fragile; platform UI changes can break functionality without notice
Some platforms require manual cookie/login configuration, reducing the zero-config promise
No Docker support or examples directory for easier onboarding
useCases: AI agents performing cross-platform social media research and sentiment analysis
Automated content monitoring and aggregation from YouTube, Reddit, and Twitter
Competitive intelligence gathering across Chinese and Western social platforms
curation.targetAudience: AI agent users (Claude Code, Cursor, Windsurf, OpenClaw) who want to extend their agent's capabilities to read and search the internet without paying for API access
curation.oneLiner: Gives any AI agent eyes to see the entire internet through one CLI with zero API fees and self-healing multi-backend routing.

name: page-agent
description: JavaScript in-page GUI agent. Control web interfaces with natural language.

curation.summary: Page Agent is a JavaScript in-page GUI agent that enables natural language control of web interfaces without requiring browser extensions, Python, or headless browsers. It uses text-based DOM manipulation rather than screenshots, allowing integration with any LLM.
curation.whyNotable: It offers a uniquely lightweight approach to web GUI automation by running entirely in-page with just JavaScript, avoiding the overhead of headless browsers or multi-modal LLMs. Backed by Alibaba with rapid release cycles and strong community adoption (22k stars).
strengths: Zero-dependency in-page JavaScript integration
Text-based DOM manipulation avoids screenshot/multi-modal overhead
Bring-your-own-LLM flexibility
Active development with 33 releases in 6 months
limitations: No automated tests
No Docker support or examples directory
Relies on external LLM APIs which may introduce latency/costs
useCases: Embedding AI copilots into SaaS products
Automating form filling in enterprise systems (ERP/CRM)
Making web applications accessible via natural language
Multi-page browser automation via Chrome extension
curation.targetAudience: Web developers and product teams wanting to add AI-powered natural language control to their web applications without backend rewrites
curation.oneLiner: In-page JavaScript GUI agent that controls web interfaces with natural language using text-based DOM manipulation instead of screenshots

name: Anthropic-Cybersecurity-Skills
description: 817 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents · Mapped to 6 frameworks: MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, NIST AI RMF & MITRE F3 (Fight Fraud) · agentskills.io standard · Works with Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Codex CLI, Cursor, Gemini CLI & 20+ platforms · 29 security domains · Apache 2.0

curation.summary: A comprehensive open-source library of 817 structured cybersecurity skills for AI agents, mapped to 6 major security frameworks (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, NIST AI RMF, MITRE F3) and compatible with 26+ AI platforms including Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Gemini CLI. The project follows the agentskills.io standard and covers 29 security domains spanning offensive, defensive, and governance use cases.
curation.whyNotable: This is the largest open-source cybersecurity skills library for AI agents, with exceptional breadth (817 skills, 29 domains) and deep framework integration (6 major cybersecurity frameworks). Its cross-platform compatibility with 26+ AI coding and agent tools makes it a unique bridge between cybersecurity expertise and AI agent capabilities, enabling security automation at scale.
strengths: Unmatched breadth with 817 skills across 29 security domains
Deep framework mapping to 6 major cybersecurity standards (MITRE ATT&CK, NIST CSF 2.0, MITRE ATLAS, D3FEND, NIST AI RMF, MITRE F3)
Broad platform compatibility with 26+ AI agent platforms
Active maintenance with 4 releases in 6 months and CI pipeline
Well-structured README with clear navigation and comprehensive documentation
limitations: No automated tests to validate skill correctness or consistency
No Docker support or examples for quick evaluation
Community project without official Anthropic affiliation, which may affect long-term sustainability
Offensive/dual-use content requires careful governance and authorized use policies
useCases: Automating penetration testing and red team operations via AI agents
Building AI-assisted incident response and threat hunting workflows
Generating security documentation and compliance mappings aligned to NIST frameworks
Creating AI-powered security training and simulation environments
Integrating cybersecurity capabilities into AI coding assistants for DevSecOps
curation.targetAudience: Cybersecurity professionals, red teamers, security engineers, and AI agent developers who want to embed structured security expertise into AI-powered workflows and tools
comparableProjects: OWASP AI Security and Privacy Guide
curation.oneLiner: The largest open-source cybersecurity skill library for AI agents, mapping 817 security capabilities across 6 frameworks to 26+ AI platforms

name: palmier-pro
description: macOS video editor built for AI

curation.summary: Palmier Pro is an open-source, Swift-native macOS video editor designed with deep AI integration at its core. It combines traditional timeline-based video editing with built-in generative AI capabilities (Seedance, Kling, Nano Banana Pro) and exposes an MCP server allowing AI agents like Claude, Codex, and Cursor to directly manipulate video projects collaboratively.
curation.whyNotable: The project pioneers an MCP-first approach to creative software, enabling AI agents to co-edit video timelines directly — a novel paradigm for human-AI collaboration in content creation. Backed by Y Combinator S24 and built from scratch in Swift for macOS, it bridges the gap between professional video editing tools and the emerging AI agent ecosystem.
strengths: Deep MCP server integration allows multiple AI coding agents (Claude, Codex, Cursor, Claude Desktop) to interact with the video editor programmatically
Built natively in Swift for macOS with Apple Silicon optimization, providing strong performance for a video editing application
Extremely active development pace with 60 releases in 6 months, 15 contributors, and nearly 10K stars indicating strong community interest
Multilingual README support (16 languages) demonstrates attention to global developer and user accessibility
limitations: No automated test suite despite CI presence, raising concerns about regression safety and long-term code quality
Tightly coupled to macOS ecosystem (requires macOS 26 Tahoe on Apple Silicon), severely limiting cross-platform adoption
useCases: AI-assisted video editing where users can instruct an agent to generate, cut, or rearrange timeline content via natural language
Programmatic video production workflows where developers build automated video pipelines using MCP-connected agents
Rapid video content creation leveraging built-in generative AI models for B-roll, transitions, and visual effects
curation.targetAudience: Content creators, video editors, and developers who want to integrate AI agents into video production workflows, particularly those in the macOS and Apple ecosystem
curation.oneLiner: A Swift-native macOS video editor that exposes an MCP server, letting AI agents like Claude directly edit timelines alongside humans.

name: agent-native
description: A framework for building agent-native applications.

curation.summary: Agent-Native is an open-source TypeScript framework for building applications where agents and UI share the same actions, state, and context. It enables developers to define work once and use it across every app surface including UI, agent, HTTP, MCP, A2A, and CLI, with a built-in agent runtime featuring chat, tools, skills, memory, jobs, observability, and handoffs.
curation.whyNotable: The framework's core innovation is unifying actions across all app surfaces—UI, agents, HTTP, MCP, A2A, and CLI—eliminating duplication between frontend and agent logic. Backed by BuilderIO with 38 contributors, 100 releases in 6 months, and 3,396 stars, it demonstrates strong momentum and a clear vision for agent-native architecture.
strengths: Unified action model across UI, agent, HTTP, MCP, A2A, and CLI reduces code duplication
Strong development velocity with 100 releases in 6 months and 38 contributors
Backend agnostic with Drizzle SQL and Nitro-compatible host support
Comprehensive agent runtime with chat, tools, skills, memory, jobs, observability, and handoffs included
limitations: No automated tests despite active development and CI presence
No Docker support for containerized deployments
Relatively new project (created March 2026) with limited production track record
useCases: Building AI-powered applications where agents and users interact with the same underlying actions
Creating coding agents with visual planning and recap capabilities
Developing content management tools with agent-assisted drafting and publishing
curation.targetAudience: Full-stack TypeScript developers building AI-integrated applications who want agents and UI to share the same action layer
curation.oneLiner: A TypeScript framework where agents and UI share the same actions, state, and context—define work once, use it everywhere.

name: agent-toolkit-for-aws
description: Official, AWS-supported MCP servers, skills, and plugins to help AI agents build on AWS

curation.summary: The Agent Toolkit for AWS is an official AWS project providing MCP servers, skills, and plugins that enable AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Kiro) to build, deploy, and manage applications on AWS. It offers comprehensive coverage of AWS services including CDK, serverless, containers, storage, observability, and AI agent development with Bedrock.
curation.whyNotable: As an official AWS-supported project with GA status, it provides standardized, maintained tooling for integrating AWS services into popular AI coding agents, potentially becoming the canonical way developers interact with AWS through AI assistants.
strengths: Official AWS backing and support with GA status
Multi-platform support across major AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Kiro)
Comprehensive AWS service coverage including infrastructure, analytics, and AI/ML services
Active development with 32 contributors and recent commits
limitations: No automated tests despite GA status
No formal releases or Docker support
No examples or tutorials provided
Very new project with limited track record
useCases: AI-assisted AWS infrastructure development and deployment
Building and managing AI agents on Amazon Bedrock
Automated data analytics workflows with AWS Glue and Athena
DevSecOps automation with AI coding agents
curation.targetAudience: Developers and teams using AI coding agents who need to build, deploy, and manage applications on AWS
curation.oneLiner: Official AWS plugins that give AI coding agents native capabilities to build and deploy on AWS

name: cognee
description: Cognee is the open-source AI memory platform for agents. Give your AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions with a self-hosted knowledge graph engine.

curation.summary: Cognee is an open-source AI memory platform that gives AI agents persistent long-term memory across sessions using a self-hosted knowledge graph engine. It ingests data in any format, builds structured knowledge graphs, and enables agents to recall, connect, and act with full context. The project combines vector databases with graph databases to create a cognitive memory layer for AI applications.
curation.whyNotable: Cognee addresses a critical gap in AI agent development—persistent, structured memory across sessions—using a novel knowledge graph approach rather than simple vector storage. With 26,742 stars, 191 contributors, and 48 releases in six months, it has rapidly grown into a significant open-source project in the agent infrastructure space. Its self-hosted nature appeals to enterprises concerned about data privacy and vendor lock-in.
strengths: Combines knowledge graphs with vector databases for rich, structured memory representation
Self-hosted architecture gives users full data control and privacy
Highly active development with 48 releases in 6 months and 191 contributors
Apache-2.0 license enables commercial use without restrictions
Strong community ecosystem with Discord, Reddit, and community plugins
limitations: No automated tests configured, raising reliability concerns for production use
223 open issues suggest the project is still maturing and may have stability gaps
Knowledge graph complexity may introduce unnecessary overhead for simple memory use cases
useCases: Building AI assistants that remember user preferences and past conversations across sessions
Enterprise knowledge management with persistent, queryable agent memory
Multi-session AI applications requiring contextual continuity and reasoning
Research into cognitive architectures and memory-augmented AI systems
curation.targetAudience: AI developers and teams building agent-based applications that require persistent, structured memory across sessions, particularly those prioritizing data sovereignty and self-hosting.
curation.oneLiner: Cognee gives AI agents persistent, structured long-term memory through a self-hosted knowledge graph engine, enabling context-aware interactions across sessions.

name: MediaCrawler
description: 小红书笔记 | 评论爬虫、抖音视频 | 评论爬虫、快手视频 | 评论爬虫、B 站视频 ｜ 评论爬虫、微博帖子 ｜ 评论爬虫、百度贴吧帖子 ｜ 百度贴吧评论回复爬虫  | 知乎问答文章｜评论爬虫

curation.summary: MediaCrawler is a Python-based web scraper for Chinese social media platforms such as Xiaohongshu, Douyin, and Bilibili. It uses Playwright browser automation to maintain login states and collect data like posts and comments without requiring JavaScript reverse engineering.
curation.whyNotable: The project has garnered over 54,000 GitHub stars due to its ability to scrape multiple popular Chinese platforms with reduced technical complexity, making it accessible for data collection tasks.
strengths: Supports a wide range of Chinese social media platforms including Xiaohongshu, Douyin, and Bilibili
Utilizes Playwright for browser automation, lowering the barrier for users without reverse engineering expertise
High community adoption with active contributions and CI/tests in place
limitations: Ethical and legal risks associated with web scraping, as highlighted in the disclaimer
Lacks Docker support and recent releases, indicating potential gaps in deployment and maintenance
useCases: Collecting social media data for academic research or market analysis
Aggregating user-generated content for trend monitoring
Building datasets for natural language processing or machine learning models

curation.oneLiner: A popular multi-platform social media crawler for Chinese sites that simplifies data collection through Playwright automation.

name: worldmonitor
description: Real-time global intelligence dashboard. AI-powered news aggregation, geopolitical monitoring, and infrastructure tracking in a unified situational awareness interface

curation.summary: World Monitor is a real-time global intelligence dashboard that provides AI-powered news aggregation, geopolitical monitoring, and infrastructure tracking through a unified situational awareness interface. It offers multiple specialized variants (tech, finance, commodity, energy, happy) and supports web, desktop (Windows, macOS), and self-hosted deployments. The project is highly active with 93 contributors and 43 releases in the last six months.
curation.whyNotable: With over 61,000 stars and a rapidly growing community, World Monitor has emerged as a popular open-source alternative to commercial intelligence platforms like Palantir. Its multi-variant approach allows users to tailor the dashboard to specific domains (finance, energy, commodities, etc.), while the AI-powered aggregation provides real-time insights from global sources. The project's exceptional development velocity and comprehensive CI/CD pipeline demonstrate strong community momentum.
strengths: Highly active development with 43 releases in 6 months and 93 contributors
Multiple domain-specific variants (tech, finance, commodity, energy, happy) for tailored monitoring
Comprehensive DevOps with CI, tests, Docker support, and cross-platform desktop builds

name: voicebox
description: The open-source AI voice studio. Clone, dictate, create.

curation.summary: Voicebox is a local-first, open-source AI voice studio that unifies voice cloning, text-to-speech generation across 23 languages and 7 engines, and system-wide dictation into a single desktop application. It bundles Whisper for speech recognition, Qwen3-TTS and other engines for synthesis, and a local LLM for text refinement, positioning itself as a privacy-respecting alternative to ElevenLabs and WisprFlow.
curation.whyNotable: With over 37K stars and 25 releases in six months, Voicebox has rapidly emerged as the leading open-source voice I/O stack by combining voice cloning, multilingual TTS, and global dictation into one local-first app. Its MCP integration for giving AI agents custom voices and its commitment to running entirely on-device make it a compelling privacy-first alternative to cloud-dependent competitors.
strengths: Local-first architecture ensures privacy and eliminates cloud dependency
Unified voice I/O stack combining cloning, TTS, and dictation in one app
Broad engine support with 7 TTS engines and 23 languages
Active development with 49 contributors and 25 releases in 6 months
MCP integration enables giving AI agents custom voices
limitations: No automated tests despite production ambitions
No examples directory for onboarding new users
Relatively new project with limited long-term stability track record
useCases: Voice cloning for content creators and podcasters
System-wide dictation into any application via global hotkey
Multilingual text-to-speech generation for accessibility and content
Giving MCP-aware AI agents a custom, consistent voice
Privacy-focused voice I/O for users avoiding cloud services
curation.targetAudience: Content creators, developers building AI agents, privacy-conscious users, and anyone seeking an open-source alternative to ElevenLabs or WisprFlow
curation.oneLiner: A local-first, open-source AI voice studio that unifies voice cloning, multilingual TTS, and system-wide dictation into a single privacy-respecting desktop app.

name: penpot
description: Penpot: The open-source design platform for Product teams that need scalable collaboration.

curation.summary: Penpot is an open-source design and prototyping platform that enables teams to collaborate on UI/UX design with full ownership of their design infrastructure, supporting self-hosting and web-based workflows. It is built with Clojure and ClojureScript, offering a robust alternative to proprietary design tools like Figma and Sketch.
curation.whyNotable: Penpot stands out as a mature, community-driven open-source alternative to proprietary design tools, with strong adoption (54k+ stars), active development (17 releases in 6 months), and a focus on design-code collaboration and self-hosting.
strengths: Open-source with full ownership and self-hosting capabilities
Large and active community (54k+ stars, 327 contributors)
Regular releases and active maintenance
limitations: No built-in AI features or AI integration mentioned
Lacks tests and Docker support in the repository metadata
Learning curve for Clojure-based customization
useCases: UI/UX design and prototyping for web and mobile apps
Collaborative design workflows for distributed teams
Self-hosted design infrastructure for organizations with data sovereignty needs
curation.targetAudience: Design teams, UX professionals, and organizations seeking an open-source, self-hosted alternative to proprietary design tools like Figma or Sketch.
curation.oneLiner: The leading open-source design platform for collaborative UI/UX design and prototyping with full self-hosting ownership.

name: container
description: A tool for creating and running Linux containers using lightweight virtual machines on a Mac. It is written in Swift, and optimized for Apple silicon.

curation.summary: apple/container is an open-source tool from Apple for running Linux containers on Apple silicon Macs using lightweight virtual machines. It is OCI-compatible, written in Swift, and leverages macOS virtualization features for native performance.
curation.whyNotable: It provides a native, high-performance container runtime optimized for Apple silicon, offering a compelling alternative to Docker Desktop with tight integration into macOS and frequent releases backed by Apple.
strengths: Native Apple silicon optimization with lightweight VM-based isolation
Full OCI image compatibility, enabling pull/push from standard registries
Active development with 8 releases in the last 6 months and 98 contributors
Clear documentation and guided tutorials for onboarding
limitations: No automated tests visible in the repository, raising concerns about regression safety
Requires macOS 26, excluding users on older macOS versions
Relatively new project (created May 2025) with a small but growing fork count

CI/CD pipelines running on Apple silicon hardware
Building and sharing OCI images in a Mac-native workflow
curation.targetAudience: Developers and DevOps engineers using Apple silicon Macs who need a fast, native container runtime without the overhead of traditional hypervisors.

name: last30days-skill
description: AI agent skill that researches any topic across Reddit, X, YouTube, HN, Polymarket, and the web - then synthesizes a grounded summary

curation.summary: last30days-skill is an AI agent plugin that performs parallel research across Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, Hacker News, Polymarket, and the web, then synthesizes results into a grounded brief ranked by real engagement metrics like upvotes, likes, and market odds. Designed as a universal skill for 50+ AI agent hosts including Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot, and Gemini CLI, it bridges the walled gardens of social platforms that no single search engine or AI can access alone. The project has gained massive traction with nearly 48k stars and a vibrant contributor community.
curation.whyNotable: The project solves a genuinely unique problem: no single AI assistant can natively search Reddit, X, TikTok, YouTube, and Polymarket simultaneously. By packaging this as a portable agent skill with zero-config defaults, it enables a fundamentally new kind of research workflow—scoring information by what real people engage with rather than editorial algorithms or SEO rankings.
strengths: Bridges dozens of walled-garden platforms (Reddit, X, TikTok, YouTube, HN, Polymarket) into a single unified search, which no existing AI or search engine can do natively
Engagement-based scoring (upvotes, likes, real money on Polymarket) rather than editorial/SEO-based ranking provides genuinely novel signal
Exceptionally broad compatibility with 50+ AI agent hosts and easy zero-config installation, making it immediately usable across the ecosystem
limitations: Requires users to provide their own API keys and browser sessions for several platforms, creating setup friction for full functionality
Dependent on external platform APIs and scraping methods that are subject to breakage, rate limiting, or policy changes from X, TikTok, Reddit, etc.
useCases: Preparing for meetings by researching a person's recent activities, projects, and public engagement across social platforms
Tracking trending topics and public sentiment across multiple social and financial prediction markets simultaneously
Deep research on niche topics where the best signals live in community discussions (Reddit, HN) rather than traditional search results
curation.targetAudience: Developers and knowledge workers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Copilot) who need multi-platform research capabilities; analysts and researchers who track trends across social media and prediction markets
curation.oneLiner: An agent skill that bridges Reddit, X, YouTube, TikTok, HN, and Polymarket into a single engagement-scored research search no other AI can provide alone

Regularly updated with new models and version diffs
Well-organized directory structure with clear navigation
Strong community engagement with 21 contributors and active PRs
Featured in major media (Washington Post) for AI transparency
limitations: No automated extraction or verification pipeline — relies on manual community contributions

Quality and completeness depend on individual contributors' extraction methods
No API or programmatic access to the collected prompts

Prompt engineering education and reverse-engineering
Tracking how AI companies change system instructions across model versions
Understanding AI assistant capabilities and constraints
Academic study of AI alignment and instruction-following behavior
curation.targetAudience: AI researchers, prompt engineers, AI safety investigators, educators, journalists, and curious developers who want to understand how major AI chatbots are configured
curation.oneLiner: The most comprehensive public archive of leaked system prompts from every major AI chatbot, serving as an essential transparency and education resource for the AI community.

name: herdr
description: agent multiplexer that lives in your terminal.

curation.summary: Herdr is a terminal multiplexer purpose-built for orchestrating multiple AI coding agents. It gives each agent its own real terminal pane, displays agent states (blocked/working/done/idle) in a sidebar, and supports workspaces, tabs, and mouse-native pane management — all as a single ~10MB Rust binary that persists sessions across detaches and works over SSH.
curation.whyNotable: Herdr fills a genuine gap between generic terminal multiplexers (tmux) and GUI agent managers by combining tmux-like persistence and lightweight operation with native agent awareness. With 10K+ stars, 65 releases in 6 months, and 34 contributors, it has rapidly become the go-to tool for developers running multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) simultaneously, offering real terminal rendering, at-a-glance status, and a scriptable socket API.
strengths: Lightweight single Rust binary (~10MB) with no dependencies, runs on Linux and macOS
Agent state awareness with sidebar showing blocked/working/done/idle status at a glance
Real terminal per agent supports full-screen TUIs, not app imitations
Persistent sessions with detach/reattach from any terminal, including over SSH
Mouse-native UI with workspaces, tabs, and drag-to-split panes
Scriptable via local socket API and CLI with multi-language plugin support
Very active development: 65 releases in 6 months, 34 contributors, CI and tests
limitations: No Docker support by design, limiting containerized deployment workflows
No examples directory to help new users get started quickly
Windows support is still in beta
Relatively new project (created March 2026) with limited long-term track record
useCases: Orchestrating multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, etc.) in parallel within a single terminal
Remote development with persistent agent sessions that survive laptop sleep and network changes via SSH
Organizing agent work across multiple repositories or projects using workspaces and tabs
curation.targetAudience: Developers and engineers who use multiple AI coding agents simultaneously and need a lightweight, terminal-native way to monitor, orchestrate, and persist agent sessions
curation.oneLiner: A tmux-like terminal multiplexer rebuilt from the ground up for orchestrating and monitoring multiple AI coding agents with real terminal views and at-a-glance status.

name: freellmapi
description: OpenAI-compatible proxy that stacks the free tiers of 16 LLM providers (~1.7B tokens/month) behind one /v1 endpoint — plus any custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Smart routing, automatic failover, encrypted keys. Personal experimentation only.

curation.summary: FreeLLMAPI is an OpenAI-compatible proxy that aggregates the free tiers of 16+ LLM providers (~1.7B tokens/month, 161 models) behind a single /v1 endpoint. It features smart routing, automatic failover across providers, encrypted API key storage, and per-key usage tracking to stay within free-tier limits.
curation.whyNotable: It solves a real pain point—managing dozens of free-tier LLM providers with different SDKs, rate limits, and failure modes—by collapsing them into one familiar OpenAI-compatible interface. With ~15K stars and 39 contributors, it has significant community traction and offers a practical way to access substantial inference capacity at zero cost.
strengths: Aggregates 16+ free LLM providers behind a single OpenAI-compatible endpoint, eliminating SDK fragmentation
Smart routing with automatic failover when providers hit rate limits or go down
Well-documented README with clear architecture explanation, Docker support, and CI pipeline
Encrypted key storage and per-key usage tracking to manage free-tier caps across providers
limitations: No automated tests despite having CI, limiting confidence in reliability
Explicitly labeled as personal experimentation only, not intended for production workloads
Free catalog lags behind premium by 30 days and 79 models, creating a paywall for timely access
Reliant on third-party free tiers which can change or disappear without notice
useCases: Personal developers experimenting with multiple LLM providers without managing separate API keys and SDKs
Prototyping and development where cost-free inference across diverse models is needed
Building applications that need resilience through automatic failover across multiple LLM backends
curation.targetAudience: Individual developers, hobbyists, and researchers who want free access to a wide variety of LLM providers through a single familiar interface without managing multiple integrations.
curation.oneLiner: A self-hosted OpenAI-compatible proxy that stacks 16+ free LLM provider tiers into one endpoint with smart routing and automatic failover.

Multiple output formats including SARIF for integration with security tools
limitations: No releases in the last 6 months, suggesting limited release cadence
No examples provided in the repository, which may hinder quick adoption
Relies on LLM for semantic analysis, which may introduce cost or latency

Continuous integration pipeline integration to vet skills before deployment
Security audits of existing agent skill collections in enterprise environments

name: LMCache
description: LMCache: Supercharge Your LLM with the Fastest KV Cache Layer

curation.summary: LMCache is a high-performance KV Cache management layer for LLM inference that caches and reuses key-value pairs across requests to dramatically accelerate inference and reduce compute costs. It integrates with popular serving frameworks like vLLM and supports multi-node P2P CPU memory sharing, CUDA, ROCm, and AMD hardware.
curation.whyNotable: LMCache addresses one of the most critical bottlenecks in LLM serving — KV cache memory and computation redundancy — with a mature, rapidly evolving solution that has gained significant industry traction including NVIDIA Dynamo integration, PyTorch Foundation membership, and production deployments with CoreWeave and Cohere.
strengths: Active development with 35 releases in 6 months and 239 contributors
Strong industry adoption: NVIDIA Dynamo integration, PyTorch Foundation member, production deployments with CoreWeave and Cohere
Multi-platform support across CUDA, ROCm, and AMD hardware
Innovative features like multi-node P2P CPU memory sharing and multiprocess architecture for MoE inference
limitations: Relatively new project (created May 2024) with 123 open issues indicating ongoing maturation
Requires integration with existing serving infrastructure like vLLM, adding deployment complexity
useCases: Accelerating high-volume LLM inference in production serving environments
Reducing compute costs for multi-tenant LLM deployments through KV cache reuse
Optimizing multi-turn agentic workloads where conversation context can be cached
Cross-node KV cache sharing in distributed LLM serving clusters
curation.targetAudience: ML infrastructure engineers and platform teams deploying LLM inference at scale who need to optimize throughput, latency, and cost
curation.oneLiner: A KV Cache management layer that supercharges LLM inference by intelligently caching and reusing key-value pairs across requests, reducing redundant computation and memory usage.

No examples directory or detailed usage tutorials
Limited to GGUF model format based on project topics

curation.summary: OmniRoute is a free AI gateway that provides a single endpoint to access 237+ AI providers (90+ free), enabling developers to connect coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, and Copilot to free Claude, GPT, and Gemini models. It features proprietary RTK+Caveman token compression (15-95% savings), smart auto-fallback across providers, MCP/A2A support, and multimodal APIs, aggregating approximately 1.6B+ free tokens monthly across free tiers.
curation.whyNotable: OmniRoute stands out for its unprecedented scale of free provider aggregation (90+ free tiers), innovative token compression technology that dramatically reduces costs, and seamless integration with popular AI coding assistants. With 10.5K+ stars, 223 contributors, and 100 releases in 6 months, it has rapidly become a go-to solution for developers seeking to eliminate AI API costs while maintaining reliability through intelligent fallback mechanisms.
strengths: Massive provider aggregation with 237+ providers and 90+ free tiers
Proprietary RTK+Caveman compression saving 15-95% tokens automatically
Broad compatibility with Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Codex, Copilot, and more
Smart auto-fallback ensures reliability when providers fail or hit limits
Active development with 223 contributors, CI/CD, Docker support, and 100 releases in 6 months
limitations: Reliance on free tiers which can change, disappear, or impose rate limits without notice
Potential latency overhead from routing requests through an additional gateway layer
Complexity of managing and configuring 237+ providers may overwhelm some users
useCases: Developers using AI coding assistants who want to eliminate API costs by leveraging free tiers
Teams needing reliable AI access with automatic fallback when providers fail or hit limits
Cost-conscious developers and startups experimenting with multiple AI models without financial commitment
Developers seeking to avoid vendor lock-in by routing requests across many providers
curation.targetAudience: Developers using AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Codex) who want free or low-cost AI inference, and teams seeking reliable multi-provider AI access with automatic failover
curation.oneLiner: A free AI gateway aggregating 90+ free provider tiers with proprietary token compression, letting developers run Claude Code, Cursor, and other AI coding tools at zero cost with smart auto-fallback.

Its key differentiator is native MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration and pre-built agent skills, enabling AI agents like Claude Code to directly access and act on SEO data.
curation.whyNotable: It bridges the gap between traditional SEO tools and the AI agent ecosystem by offering first-class MCP support and reusable agent skills, making SEO workflows programmable and agent-native while maintaining a self-hostable, open-source model with no subscription lock-in.
strengths: Native MCP server integration for AI agents with pre-built skills
Open-source and self-hostable with pay-as-you-go pricing via DataForSEO
Active development with 21 releases in 6 months and growing community
Focused, modern UI avoiding bloat of traditional SEO suites
limitations: No automated tests despite having CI
No Docker support or examples despite documentation mentioning them
Relies entirely on third-party DataForSEO API, creating cost and dependency risks
Very new project with only 5 contributors and limited track record

Developers building AI agent workflows that require SEO data and analysis
Marketing teams wanting to integrate SEO insights into AI-powered content pipelines
Agencies needing customizable, white-label SEO tooling without subscription fees

name: astryx
description: An open source design system that's fully customizable and agent ready

curation.summary: Astryx is an open-source design system from Meta (Facebook) that provides 150+ accessible React components, brand-level theming, dark mode, and a CLI. It differentiates itself with 'open internals' (composable building blocks, swizzle-to-eject pattern), no styling lock-in (works with Tailwind, CSS modules, or plain CSS), and an API/docs/CLI designed for both human developers and AI coding assistants to build from the same reference.
curation.whyNotable: Astryx is notable as Meta's most-used internal design system (powering 13,000+ apps over 8 years) being open-sourced with a deliberate 'agent-ready' philosophy — its CLI, component API, and documentation are co-designed so AI coding assistants can build alongside humans using the same tooling. The swizzle-to-eject pattern and styling-agnostic approach also address long-standing pain points in design system adoption.
strengths: Agent-ready design: CLI, API, and docs are co-designed for AI assistants and humans to build from the same reference
No styling lock-in: works with Tailwind, CSS modules, or plain CSS via className overrides
Open internals with swizzle-to-eject pattern: full component source can be ejected into a project for full ownership
Battle-tested at scale: powers 13,000+ apps inside Meta over 8 years
limitations: Currently in Beta with no automated tests in the open-source repository
No Docker support or official examples directory for quick prototyping
Relatively new open-source project (created Jan 2026) with a small contributor base (32) compared to established design systems
useCases: Building consistent, accessible UIs across large organizations with multiple teams and apps
Enabling AI coding assistants (Cursor, Copilot, etc.) to generate UI code using a shared component vocabulary
White-labeling or rebranding applications via CSS custom property theme overrides without forking component source
Gradual adoption in existing projects without requiring build plugin or styling library changes
curation.targetAudience: Frontend engineering teams at mid-to-large organizations, design system teams, and developers who use AI coding assistants and want a component library optimized for agent-assisted workflows
curation.oneLiner: Meta's battle-tested design system open-sourced with an agent-ready philosophy — built so humans and AI assistants build UIs from the same reference.
